name: cantrip
description: A personal collection of practical Claude Code skills — small capabilities you summon at will.

plugins.name: format-diary
plugins.description: Turn an Obsidian daily note of raw saved links into a formatted diary entry: speaking titles, resolved real URLs, summaries, transcript summaries for YouTube, hashtags, and collapsed callouts holding the full page text or transcript.

plugins.name: council
plugins.description: Get an independent second opinion before acting on a plan, decision, code review, or audit. Sends the artifact to codex (an external model, read-only) plus a separate independent Claude critique, reconciles both against the actual code, and reports a verdict — consensus, conflicts, and dismissed false positives. Two modes: `council:council` runs a thorough multi-pass loop, expands surviving disagreements into an explored option map, and interactively arbitrates judgment calls with the human; `council:fast` does a quick single autonomous pass. Requires the codex CLI.

plugins.name: land-pr
plugins.description: Land a GitHub PR end-to-end in an isolated worktree — verify CI is green and review threads are resolved, run the test suite, merge, update the base branch, and clean up. Aborts at any failing gate and never merges unless every check passes.

plugins.name: grill-me
plugins.description: Interview the user relentlessly to lock a plan, then have codex adversarially review it round by round until it approves or you sign off. One question at a time, each with a recommended answer, settles the decision tree into PLAN.md; codex (an external model, read-only, over MCP) then attacks the plan across rounds while Claude arbitrates revisions, appending the exchange to PLAN-REVIEW-LOG.md. No code is written until you sign off. Requires the codex CLI.
